label: Symbolic Regression
description: Compressed-statistics symbolic regression rewritten into the network ODE form dx_i/dt = f(x_i) + sum_j W_ij G(x_i,x_j; m_i, v_i), keeping edge weights explicit to match the notation used in the site.

interpretation: The decomposition separates a simple intrinsic decay term $f(x_i)$ from a weighted network drive $\sum_j W_{ij}G(x_i,x_j; m_i)$, so the equation reads as local relaxation plus incoming circuit modulation.
The negative $x_j$ contribution inside $G$ means uniform presynaptic co-activation is not the dominant excitatory mechanism in this model.
The positive $(x_j-m_i)^2$ term shows that heterogeneous upstream activity, rather than the mean input alone, is the main driver of downstream change.

variables.name: A_{ij}
variables.description: Directed edge weight from neuron $j$ to neuron $i$ in the inferred functional network.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight, defined from $A_{ij}$ so that incoming weights to neuron $i$ sum to one.

variables.name: m_i
variables.description: Weighted mean incoming activity of neuron $i$.

variables.name: f(x_i)
variables.description: Intrinsic self-dynamics term for neuron $i$.

variables.name: G(x_i,x_j; m_i)
variables.description: Edge-local network contribution before weighting by $W_{ij}$ in the symbolic-regression rewrite.

interpretation: This result has the same overall structure as the 200-node Gaussian-TE model: intrinsic decay in $f(x_i)$ plus a heterogeneity-sensitive incoming network term.
The edge term again down-weights simple bulk co-activation and emphasizes deviations from the local incoming mean $m_i$.
That consistency across two network families suggests the dominant symbolic-regression signal is not method-specific noise, but a recurring motif of input heterogeneity shaping neural dynamics.

variables.name: A_{ij}
variables.description: Directed edge weight from neuron $j$ to neuron $i$ in the inferred functional network.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight, defined from $A_{ij}$ so that incoming weights to neuron $i$ sum to one.

variables.name: m_i
variables.description: Weighted mean incoming activity of neuron $i$.

variables.name: f(x_i)
variables.description: Intrinsic self-dynamics term for neuron $i$.

variables.name: G(x_i,x_j; m_i)
variables.description: Edge-local network contribution before weighting by $W_{ij}$ in the symbolic-regression rewrite.

interpretation: Here the intrinsic term $f(x_i)$ is state dependent through $K_i$, so the receiving neuron's own activity controls how responsive it is before network input is even added.
The network contribution enters only through the heterogeneity term $(x_j-m_i)^2$, not through a direct linear $x_j$ drive.
Biologically this suggests a gated regime in which target-neuron excitability and uneven presynaptic recruitment jointly shape propagation.

variables.name: A_{ij}
variables.description: Directed edge weight from neuron $j$ to neuron $i$ in the inferred functional network.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight, defined from $A_{ij}$ so that incoming weights to neuron $i$ sum to one.

variables.name: m_i
variables.description: Weighted mean incoming activity of neuron $i$.

variables.name: K_i
variables.description: Self-state-dependent gain factor appearing in the 200-node DBN-refine symbolic-regression model.

variables.name: G(x_i,x_j; m_i)
variables.description: Edge-local network contribution before weighting by $W_{ij}$ in the symbolic-regression rewrite.

interpretation: At 500 nodes the same decomposition remains valid: a compact intrinsic decay term $f(x_i)$ plus weighted incoming edge contributions.
The larger coefficient on the heterogeneity term compared with the linear $x_j$ term means unequal presynaptic recruitment is still the dominant factor.
This supports the view that the symbolic-regression model is responding more to structured upstream contrasts than to simple mean-field excitation.

variables.name: A_{ij}
variables.description: Directed edge weight from neuron $j$ to neuron $i$ in the inferred functional network.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight, defined from $A_{ij}$ so that incoming weights to neuron $i$ sum to one.

variables.name: m_i
variables.description: Weighted mean incoming activity of neuron $i$.

variables.name: f(x_i)
variables.description: Intrinsic self-dynamics term for neuron $i$.

variables.name: G(x_i,x_j; m_i)
variables.description: Edge-local network contribution before weighting by $W_{ij}$ in the symbolic-regression rewrite.

interpretation: This is the least pairwise-like symbolic-regression result: the decomposition into $f(x_i) + \sum_j W_{ij}G(x_i,x_j; m_i,v_i)$ is algebraically valid, but $G$ still depends on aggregated incoming statistics through $m_i$ and $v_i$.
Its main message is a regime-switching network effect, where the influence of incoming structure is gated by the target state and by whole-neighborhood statistics.
For reporting, this model should be described as a network ODE with explicit weights rather than a strict pairwise kernel of the form $W_{ij}g(x_i,x_j)$.

variables.name: A_{ij}
variables.description: Directed edge weight from neuron $j$ to neuron $i$ in the inferred functional network.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight, defined from $A_{ij}$ so that incoming weights to neuron $i$ sum to one.

variables.name: m_i
variables.description: Weighted mean incoming activity of neuron $i$.

variables.name: v_i
variables.description: Weighted incoming activity variance of neuron $i$, measuring heterogeneity across presynaptic neurons.

variables.name: B_i
variables.description: State- and network-dependent gain factor introduced by the 500-node joint-Granger symbolic-regression model.

variables.name: G(x_i,x_j; m_i, v_i)
variables.description: Network contribution inside the weighted sum; here it depends on aggregated neighborhood statistics and is not a strict pairwise kernel.

interpretation: This is the cleanest 500-node symbolic-regression rewrite: a simple intrinsic decay term plus a weighted network contribution with only two components.
Unlike the TE and joint-Granger 500-node formulas, it retains a modest positive linear $x_j$ drive, meaning coherent presynaptic activation can still support downstream excitation.

At the same time, the stronger heterogeneity term shows that structured differences across presynaptic neurons remain the dominant network signature, which is why this model is a strong candidate for concise reporting.
